topic: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous)

sections.title: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — Introduction and Formula
sections.hindiTitle: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — परिचय और फॉर्मूला
sections.explanation: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) is an essential building block of English grammar. Formula: Subject + V1/V1+s | am/is/are + V1+ing | have/has + V3 | have/has + been + V1+ing. Rules: Simple Present: routine/habit (I eat rice) | Present Continuous: happening now (I am eating rice) | Present Perfect: completed action with present relevance (I have eaten rice) | Present Perfect Continuous: ongoing action from past till now (I have been eating rice for an hour)
sections.formula: Subject + V1/V1+s | am/is/are + V1+ing | have/has + V3 | have/has + been + V1+ing

examples.hindi: मैं रोज़ चावल खाता हूँ।
मैं अभी खाना खा रहा हूँ।
examples.english: I am eating food right now.

examples.hindi: मैंने खाना खा लिया है।
मैं एक घंटे से पढ़ रहा हूँ।
examples.english: I have been studying for an hour.

examples.hindi: वह हर दिन काम पर जाती है।
examples.english: She goes to work every day.

examples.hindi: वह अभी काम कर रही है।

sections.title: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — Daily Life Usage
sections.hindiTitle: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — रोज़मर्रा के उपयोग
sections.explanation: Everyday sentences using Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) for daily conversation.
sections.formula: Subject + V1/V1+s | am/is/are + V1+ing | have/has + V3 | have/has + been + V1+ing

examples.hindi: उसने अपना काम पूरा कर लिया है।
वह दो घंटे से काम कर रही है।
examples.english: She has been working for two hours.

examples.hindi: वे रोज़ स्कूल जाते हैं।
examples.english: They go to school every day.

examples.hindi: वे अभी खेल रहे हैं।
उन्होंने अपना homework कर लिया है।
वे सुबह से खेल रहे हैं।
examples.english: They have been playing since morning.

sections.title: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — Office & Professional Usage
sections.hindiTitle: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — ऑफिस और पेशेवर उपयोग
sections.explanation: Practice Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) in professional and office contexts.
sections.formula: Subject + V1/V1+s | am/is/are + V1+ing | have/has + V3 | have/has + been + V1+ing

examples.hindi: मैं हर सुबह exercise करता हूँ।
मैं अभी पढ़ रहा हूँ।
मैंने यह किताब पढ़ ली है।
मैं तीन घंटे से यह किताब पढ़ रहा हूँ।
examples.english: I have been reading this book for three hours.

sections.title: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — Negative and Question Forms
sections.hindiTitle: Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous) — नकारात्मक और प्रश्न रूप
sections.explanation: How to make negative sentences and questions using Tenses Part 1 — Present Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous). Common mistakes are highlighted here.
sections.formula: Subject + V1/V1+s | am/is/are + V1+ing | have/has + V3 | have/has + been + V1+ing

examples.hindi: वह अच्छा गाता है।
वह अभी गा रहा है।
उसने एक नया गाना गाया है।
examples.english: He has sung a new song.

examples.hindi: वह आधे घंटे से गा रहा है।
examples.english: He has been singing for half an hour.
